sections.label: Lyrics
lines: I learned to carry silence like a second skin
Every word I buried echoing within
We build our walls from fear we never name
But loneliness and pride don’t heal the same
What if the weight we hold was never ours alone?
What if the cracks we hide are where the light has grown?
Be gentle now, be low in heart
Hold each other where we fall apart
Patient love, don’t let it break
We are one in every breath we take
One body, one spirit calling
One hope when the night is falling
One Lord, one faith, one flame
We are many—but we’re not separate names
I see your wounds inside the way you speak
Every guarded glance, every hidden plea
We’re stitched together by invisible threads
In every tear, in every word unsaid
So I’ll stay when it’s heavy, I won’t turn away
There’s a quiet kind of strength in choosing to stay
Be gentle now, be low in heart
Hold each other where we fall apart
Patient love, don’t let it break
We are one in every breath we take
One body, one spirit calling
One hope when the night is falling
One Lord, one faith, one flame
We are many—but we’re not separate names
Not above, not below
Not divided by the things we don’t know
Every soul, every scar
Still connected where we are
We bend—but we don’t sever
We break—but not forever
Be gentle now, be low in heart
Hold each other where we fall apart
Patient love, don’t let it fade
We are one in the bond we’ve made
One body, one spirit breathing
One truth we’ve been believing
One God through all we are
Closer than we thought we were apart
In the quiet, we align
Your broken pieces mirror mine
And somewhere in between the pain—
We become whole again
